[2018-06-20 00:09] namington: its not conclusively solved but
[2018-06-20 00:09] namington: every p1 line we looked at has a good response by p2

[2018-06-20 00:13] cactus_wren (cactus): does p2 typically have the advantage?
[2018-06-20 00:15] f300xen (f0x): a good heuristic is that p2 is better in green and red sets, and p1 is better in blue sets
[2018-06-20 00:23] crash_overlord: Oftentime their lines are more obvious rather than an inherent advantage
[2018-06-20 00:23] crash_overlord: but sometimes that's also true
[2018-06-20 00:23] crash_overlord: Yes p2 wins ~52% of the time if stats are still correct
[2018-06-20 00:24] camzace: its important to note that 52% isn't really inherently much - any difference in skill will skew the p1/p2 win rate a lot more
[2018-06-20 00:24] camzace: you can still claim it's why you lost
[2018-06-20 00:24] crash_overlord: Ofc, rng is always the reason I lose
[2018-06-20 00:25] crash_overlord: I don't lose for any other reason
[2018-06-20 00:26] camzace: lose as player 1, "that was clearly a player 2 favoured set"
[2018-06-20 00:26] camzace: lose as player 2, "that was clearly a player 1 favoured set"
[2018-06-20 00:26] camzace: you can even say autowin instead of favoured if you really want to double down
[2018-06-20 00:26] crash_overlord: Tbh every set is an autowin
[2018-06-20 00:26] cactus_wren (cactus): i mean i play a lot of chess
[2018-06-20 00:27] cactus_wren (cactus): the advantage is only pronounced at a high level
[2018-06-20 00:27] namington: but yeah thats true in mata too
[2018-06-20 00:27] cactus_wren (cactus): the better player just wins every game when you're not a titled player
[2018-06-20 00:27] cactus_wren (cactus): yeah that's what i'm sayihng
[2018-06-20 00:27] cactus_wren (cactus): like a 52% winrate for p2 is just not significant enough
[2018-06-20 00:27] cactus_wren (cactus): how do y'all decide how many drones to cap at?
[2018-06-20 00:28] cactus_wren (cactus): i usually look at the biggest blocker in the set
[2018-06-20 00:28] crash_overlord: Yep, same holds true here. Prismata does have a "problem" if you can call it that that a few units lead themselves to much more advantageous sets, that are much more than just "52%" favoured
[2018-06-20 00:28] crash_overlord: But they're rare and even when they're present it's not always true
[2018-06-20 00:29] cactus_wren (cactus): yeah i played a set the other day with savior, omega spitter, and energy matrix
[2018-06-20 00:29] cactus_wren (cactus): and p1 just won almost deterministically

[2018-06-20 00:29] crash_overlord: P2 gets saviour first
[2018-06-20 00:29] cactus_wren (cactus): maybe he just suckedl mao
[2018-06-20 00:30] crash_overlord: p2 fast saviour is the saviour line in saviour sets
[2018-06-20 00:30] cactus_wren (cactus): oh he mirrored my 3rd engineer
[2018-06-20 00:30] cactus_wren (cactus): is that a mistake as p2
[2018-06-20 00:31] cactus_wren (cactus): ok also i have a lot of trouble deciding when to mirror the dde opening on t2
[2018-06-20 00:31] f300xen (f0x): the savior undercut is dd dde dd(savior) for p2
[2018-06-20 00:32] cactus_wren (cactus): like 1. DD ...DD 2. DDE ...DA? ...DDE? ...DD?
[2018-06-20 00:32] cactus_wren (cactus): like is floating 4 gold turn 2

[2018-06-20 00:36] f300xen (f0x): that's the standard line usually

[2018-06-20 00:48] cactus_wren (cactus): i found out very quickly that the "bank a bunch of green and buy one big thing" is almost never good
[2018-06-20 00:49] cactus_wren (cactus): like it's just so important to use all your resources you're just begging to lose if you don't
[2018-06-20 00:50] cactus_wren (cactus): hey so if i go fastimus and they respond with blastforge, is it better to buy tarsirs or grimbots? the grimbots make him buy a wall if i go tarseers he can get spitter
[2018-06-20 00:51] namington: generally, lfespan attackers arent super great early on
[2018-06-20 00:51] willflame: usually i would buy Tarsiers because next turn i'd Rhino to absorb, and then they end up having to build a wall anyway or hold their Steelsplitter
[2018-06-20 00:51] namington: theres both the obvious reason for it (permanent attack > temporary attack when theres still a lot of turns left)
[2018-06-20 00:52] namington: and the fact that a lot of grimbotch's value comes from soaking on its last turn of life
[2018-06-20 00:52] namington: and if they're not attacking you yet, that's not super useful, so you lose value in that case
[2018-06-20 00:52] namington: generally you go for tarsiers, forcing your opponent to build walls to absorb botch isnt really worth the inefficiency
[2018-06-20 00:53] namington: considering theyd just wall next turn anyway
[2018-06-20 00:53] miuna6933 (Aka): if they can wall you don't want to do only 2 damage, you want to jump from 0 to 3-4
[2018-06-20 00:53] cactus_wren (cactus): is chrono filter busted?
[2018-06-20 00:54] cactus_wren (cactus): and i think i should hve
[2018-06-20 00:54] extratricky: Have you tried getting one of each? Sometimes two tarsiers will be better but the middle ground can be good (it's the standard if botch is replaced by vore)
[2018-06-20 00:54] namington: in most sets its unplayable
[2018-06-20 00:54] miuna6933 (Aka): chrono is very situational
[2018-06-20 00:54] namington: in a select few sets (eg wincer) its super good
[2018-06-20 00:54] cactus_wren (cactus): he's just making hannibulls
[2018-06-20 00:54] namington: and in other cases it's "just another tech building"
[2018-06-20 00:54] cactus_wren (cactus): i regret my choices this game
[2018-06-20 00:54] cactus_wren (cactus): oh god it's a wincer hannibull set and i didn't get chrono
[2018-06-20 00:55] _sakuya (Sakuya): https://gyazo.com/6789a64eb74a34252432bd894a9ab3d8
Gyazo
​
[2018-06-20 00:55] _sakuya (Sakuya): so this is a low econ set right
[2018-06-20 00:55] _sakuya (Sakuya): blue would get tatsud
[2018-06-20 00:55] cactus_wren (cactus): it's a fuckin tarsier set
[2018-06-20 00:56] cactus_wren (cactus): yeah it's got vivids and tatsus
[2018-06-20 00:56] cactus_wren (cactus): so the vivids are kinda vulnurable
[2018-06-20 00:56] cactus_wren (cactus): but they also ramp you SO fast if you go like dd 1a
[2018-06-20 00:56] namington: thats one of the hardest base+5 sets ive seen
[2018-06-20 00:56] extratricky: you have to be big enough so that you can actually get tatsu if your opponent goes blue
[2018-06-20 00:57] extratricky: but not so big that you're relying on the blue units to not die to non-tatsu
[2018-06-20 00:57] extratricky: it's pretty hard to play that line properly
[2018-06-20 00:57] _sakuya (Sakuya): blue would get countered by big red
[2018-06-20 00:57] _sakuya (Sakuya): but big red would die to low econ red
[2018-06-20 00:57] supergreyman (Greyman): It's like a rock-paper-scissor set...
[2018-06-20 00:57] namington: that set is the weird intersection of "rule of thumb"s
[2018-06-20 00:58] crash_overlord: I'm pretty sure you go econ
[2018-06-20 00:58] crash_overlord: then commit to double animus
[2018-06-20 00:58] namington: as a rule of thumb, ampo is better in sets with better defense
[2018-06-20 00:58] crash_overlord: if your opp goes early A you go double B and win
[2018-06-20 00:58] namington: as a rule of thumb, tatsu is good at exploiting lifespan defense
[2018-06-20 00:58] namington: and as a rule of thumb, ampo is bad at defending
[2018-06-20 00:58] namington: well, big red is in general
[2018-06-20 00:58] namington: so you have these weird interplays
[2018-06-20 00:59] namington: the problem is if you play non-committal too long then ampo just blows you up
[2018-06-20 00:59] namington: playing non-committal is, in a way, committal
[2018-06-20 00:59] supergreyman (Greyman): Doomed Mech > regular Tarsier
Regular Tarsier > Big Red (??)
Big Red > Doomed Mech

--- Thread (42 messages, 2018-06-20) ---
[2018-06-20 00:59] extratricky: in particular regular tarsier > double A
[2018-06-20 00:59] extratricky: if they don't get double A you get kind of a base+vivid game
[2018-06-20 00:59] cactus_wren (cactus): is defense grid good early
[2018-06-20 01:00] namington: tarsiers dont beat big red + dmech but they probably beat big red + dmech if they have the option to pivot into tatsu themself
[2018-06-20 01:00] extratricky: but the econ size is warped due to the other possiblities
[2018-06-20 01:00] supergreyman (Greyman): It's good if you can absorb damage. 😄
[2018-06-20 01:00] namington: you generally only wanna get dgrid when its actually absorbing for full or near-full
[2018-06-20 01:00] namington: if your opps only doing 3 damage, then walls probably fine for now
[2018-06-20 01:00] supergreyman (Greyman): If it only defends against 1 attack, it's probably bad.
[2018-06-20 01:00] namington: but if they ramp up to 5
[2018-06-20 01:00] namington: then thats probably about time to grab dgrid
[2018-06-20 01:01] namington: its like reverse antima: you want to get it as early as it's feasible to get value out of
[2018-06-20 01:01] namington: (whereas antima you want to get it as LATE as its possible to get value from)
[2018-06-20 01:02] namington: *assuming antima doesnt just blow your opponent up, of course
[2018-06-20 01:05] _sakuya (Sakuya): ended up being base + vivid
[2018-06-20 01:05] namington: yeah thats my tendency

[2018-06-20 07:50] cogito3 (Cogito): the problem with turn 5 is not so much that you allow a breach, and more that you set up dealing 3 damage into a Shredder. Buying a Rhino instead of one Tarsier would not only have prevented the breach, but also would have enabled you to frontline one of the opponent's Shredders.
[2018-06-20 07:53] cogito3 (Cogito): allowing a breach on turn 6 to get a Tatsu here is fine, but it only really works b/c your opponent plays way too passively--getting Deadeyes instead of damage. If they had just went for Valk immediately, or even maybe spammed Shredders, you likely would have lost
[2018-06-20 07:55] cogito3 (Cogito): but yeah, if your opponent buys frontline units like shredder or hannibull, try to set it up so you deal just enough damage to kill it (as a general rule, there are always exceptions in prismata)

[2018-06-20 08:24] _sakuya (Sakuya): Considering the game got to the point where i got my own dgrid out of going fastimus i think something went wrong
[2018-06-20 08:24] _sakuya (Sakuya): Namely that i dont know when to build smorcus and when to click it
[2018-06-20 08:26] _sakuya (Sakuya): Is a turn 4 smorcus too fast?
[2018-06-20 08:27] _sakuya (Sakuya): Is it fine to use smorcus as a way to get over the min damage and reeconomy behind it?
[2018-06-20 08:36] leary93: I think smorcus doesnt have enough support tbf not early
[2018-06-20 08:36] leary93: Ebb turbine allows early blue to be good and economy up after the first wall
[2018-06-20 08:37] leary93: D-grid seems dominating the set and if you get up to 6 attack before it dgrid just gets free value
[2018-06-20 08:37] f300xen (f0x): get dgrid, get tarsiers, get smorcus
[2018-06-20 08:37] leary93: So prob go d-grid -> tarsiers -> symbiota
[2018-06-20 08:38] leary93: As theres no good soak, rhinos have a good job to fullfill
[2018-06-20 08:39] leary93: Also throw 1 or 2 ebbs in the mix so you can start consuming drones once your opp is struggling for defense
[2018-06-20 08:43] _sakuya (Sakuya): How do you properly use rhinos to do more than prompt soaking
[2018-06-20 08:44] _sakuya (Sakuya): Do you not consider them as defense the turn you build them
[2018-06-20 08:44] _sakuya (Sakuya): So that its not really overdefendingsince you werent going to use them for def until theyre out of stam?
[2018-06-20 08:45] mtanzer: rhinos aren't very good soak.  since there's smorcus you can tech out of red once attack gets too high and most of your soak can be walls and forcefields
[2018-06-20 08:45] _sakuya (Sakuya): Im awful at the rhino train thing
[2018-06-20 08:46] f300xen (f0x): maybe you can ossi everything
[2018-06-20 08:46] mtanzer: in general sometimes you do buy prompt rhinos when attack is really high.  just a bit before that when you're almost at the point of only buying defense you might consider buying rhino instead of tarsier.  at that point you can probably still click some of them once or twice
[2018-06-20 08:49] leary93: Rhino's arent good soak, but they are good worth if you need defense in a couple turns but can use attack now
[2018-06-20 08:49] leary93: Im not saying you use them as raw soak XD
[2018-06-20 08:50] mtanzer: yeah I'm saying even when used like that rhino is still mediocre and I'd rather be buying walls, forcefields, and engis (and more tarsier/smorcus now if still possible), and only rely on rhinos if I'm gonna run out of the other soak too soon
[2018-06-20 08:51] mtanzer: oh and symbiote too.  you probably want a bunch of symbiotes and you probably want to pop them when pressure gets really high

[2018-06-21 21:27] jonshea: I don’t understand Iso Kronus. It has the same average damage as Gauss Cannon, for one less gold, but with an extra turn of build time. It seems to me that inflation / lost damage on that build time is almost always going to be greater than 1 gold in value. I get that you can sync Isos to deny absorb, it that situation seems pretty rare, and I see top players sometimes buy Isos even when they have permanent damage equal to the absorb barrier. And I get that Iso makes sense when Tarsier is good, for example if damage the next turn would just be absorbed, but in two turns you expect to be over the absorb barrier. Why do people buy Isos outside those two situations? Am I missing something?
[2018-06-21 21:28] namington: well, ignoring breachproof
[2018-06-21 21:28] namington: you're right that iso is inefficient
[2018-06-21 21:28] namington: hell, its less efficient than gauss cannon
[2018-06-21 21:29] namington: but its still more efficient than floating gold + green
[2018-06-21 21:29] namington: so if iso happens to fit really smoothly into your turn when gauss cannon doesnt, its perfectly fine
[2018-06-21 21:30] namington: of course, iso's _best_ use is denying absorb
[2018-06-21 21:30] namington: it's also used in breachproof games as its basically just extra gauss cannon supply
[2018-06-21 21:30] namington: slightly worse than gauss cannon but still
[2018-06-21 21:31] jonshea: Sure, I guess I get that. If you have 5g and your only option is to float it, then I would Iso. But I would also think about shuffling my buys to find 1 gold and buy a Gauss instead.
[2018-06-21 21:31] leary93: Iso kronus actually becomes efficient when they can deny absorb, and kind of fit the same role of tarsiers, but for green, where it's a lot better as your first attacker. Of course, getting 2 right away is better than getting 1 right away, as then you immediatly go over absorb, when they become more efficient than tarsiers
[2018-06-21 21:32] leary93: But yeah, if you already have attackers that are attacking every turn, they're not efficient
[2018-06-21 21:32] leary93: same kind of holds for schorchillas
[2018-06-21 21:32] namington: its not that much worse than gauss cannon on a curve
[2018-06-21 21:32] namington: i think its like a sub-5% difference
[2018-06-21 21:32] leary93: I mean, most units are better than floating 1 gold
[2018-06-21 21:32] namington: so not really worth restructuring your turn
[2018-06-21 21:32] leary93: because all units are so close together
[2018-06-21 21:33] leary93: of course, legendary units are excepted from that rule
[2018-06-21 21:33] namington: floating any gold at all is less efficient than iso
[2018-06-21 21:33] namington: unless ofc youre saving up for something
[2018-06-21 21:33] leary93: Anyways, it is often recommended to get your first 2 iso's at once
[2018-06-21 21:34] namington: iso is also potentially good if the set doesnt have good preemptive soak
[2018-06-21 21:34] leary93: Also if there is no good slow-defense
[2018-06-21 21:34] namington: like for instance, you dont want to buy wall if its not immediately being useful
[2018-06-21 21:34] namington: thats inefficient as hell
[2018-06-21 21:34] leary93: like Iso's get hurt a lot by shredders/infusion grid
[2018-06-21 21:34] namington: so if you sync up isos, even if its not denying absorb
[2018-06-21 21:34] namington: it can make soaking awkward
[2018-06-21 21:34] leary93: because you can buy those on the off-turns to help defend
[2018-06-21 21:35] namington: and yeah thats why shredders and stuff are good vs iso
[2018-06-21 21:35] leary93: ohh wait that's what you ment
[2018-06-21 21:35] leary93: Added, good green defense also helps
[2018-06-21 21:35] leary93: like aegis/plexo, because then you can use your green once you cant buy attack very well anymore
[2018-06-21 21:35] namington: eh i mean, if isos are forcing your opponent to burn drones on plexo
[2018-06-21 21:36] namington: that's probably better value than you would've got from gausses
[2018-06-21 21:36] leary93: yeah I mean it helps for the iso
[2018-06-21 21:36] leary93: Because otherwise Iso's will just end up floating green later on
[2018-06-21 21:37] namington: I feel like on average aegis makes iso worse though
[2018-06-21 21:37] namington: like aegis makes breachproof not as good, but even without that it's just good soak
[2018-06-21 21:37] namington: and even if youre making isos you dont inherently want too many conduits (but with aegis you do)
[2018-06-21 21:38] namington: there are certainly situations where aegis just makes green so good that you get isos anyway though
[2018-06-21 21:43] leary93: Id argue you'd still want 2 conduits with iso's at some point, getting a third at that point is a low price. Also Iso's dont always have to go breachproof, that just depends on the set.

[2018-06-22 10:51] nirv9924 (Nirv): I got a kind of vague question. In sets without alternative drones and bloodrager, what's the general strategy to counter fastimus
[2018-06-22 10:52] nirv9924 (Nirv): Is there any (almost) setindependent advice ?
[2018-06-22 10:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): without blood rager or with
[2018-06-22 10:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): without you just defend
[2018-06-22 10:56] nirv9924 (Nirv): With. (Without looking at the actual numbers) I feel like I got a huge difference between being p1/p2 in rager sets
[2018-06-22 11:00] Deleted User (Deleted User): p1 can go the higher econ doublerager which can be neat
[2018-06-22 11:01] leary93: Ye I feel bloodrager allows p1 to play natural animus a bit different than he would otherwise
[2018-06-22 11:01] nirv9924 (Nirv): Natural animus TT ee or how would you start ?
[2018-06-22 11:02] leary93: Dont think you go double engi
[2018-06-22 11:02] leary93: Like, that doesnt make sense, as you only need 3 defense next turn, and just a rhino does that while setting up rager
[2018-06-22 11:02] nirv9924 (Nirv): Kind of need 1 right ?
[2018-06-22 11:03] nirv9924 (Nirv): Or the opponent can go rr
[2018-06-22 11:03] leary93: so I'd argue something like DD DD DDA TT RT (at this point you have 5 gold saved, probably build a forge?
[2018-06-22 11:03] leary93: then you can follow with W1 the turn after RT
[2018-06-22 11:03] leary93: I mean, you just hold a drone
[2018-06-22 11:03] leary93: if they double rhino
[2018-06-22 11:04] leary93: double rhino is bad for them anyways, as even the first doesnt block
[2018-06-22 11:04] nirv9924 (Nirv): It pressures you a lot though
[2018-06-22 11:04] leary93: ye but it fucks up their turns after
[2018-06-22 11:05] nirv9924 (Nirv): And holding a drone is super inefficient
[2018-06-22 11:05] leary93: if they go double rhino
[2018-06-22 11:05] leary93: that's horribly inefficient for them and your rager will probably just outpace them at that point
[2018-06-22 11:05] leary93: you just get 1 and go defensive as their attack runs out soon
[2018-06-22 11:05] leary93: if they dont continue at least some tarsiers
[2018-06-22 11:05] leary93: And they probably want to build a rager anyways
[2018-06-22 11:06] leary93: and if they dont go double rhino, you get to set up for a forge
[2018-06-22 11:06] leary93: at which point you'll have a massive advantage over them
[2018-06-22 11:06] leary93: where bloodrager helps keep them off that forge
[2018-06-22 11:07] leary93: which is the usualy issue in bso, is that p2 still gets to a forge because there's not enough pressure
[2018-06-22 11:10] mtanzer: it depends a lot on what's in the set but if you're both in red without a forge yet you don't *have to* buy rager (yet)
[2018-06-22 11:10] leary93: well that is true I suppose
[2018-06-22 11:11] leary93: especially if you are ahead in permanent attack
[2018-06-22 11:11] mtanzer: like if you're forcing them to buy prompt rhinos instead you might just want to wait til later to get it
[2018-06-22 11:12] mtanzer: if grimbotch is involved you might be able to wait until one or two are timing out since permanent attack usually stays low for example.  or if a larger absorber will be bought later (even if just wall) you often have an opportunity then
[2018-06-22 11:12] leary93: if botch is allowed, does rager even be efficient until they start running out of lifespan?
[2018-06-22 11:13] mtanzer: if rhino absorb I probably wouldn't rush out a rager in a botch game
